2603.20860.title: Restoring Neural Network Plasticity for Faster Transfer Learning
2603.20860.paper_summary: Transfer learning with models pretrained on ImageNet has become a standard practice in computer vision. Transfer learning refers to fine-tuning pretrained weights of a neural network on a downstream task, typically unrelated to ImageNet. However, pretrained weights can become saturated and may yield insignificant gradients, failing to adapt to the downstream task. This hinders the ability of the model to train effectively, and is commonly referred to as loss of neural plasticity. Loss of plasticity may prevent the model from fully adapting to the target domain, especially when the downstream dataset is atypical in nature. While this issue has been widely explored in continual learning, it remains relatively understudied in the context of transfer learning. In this work, we propose the use of a targeted weight re-initialization strategy to restore neural plasticity prior to fine-tuning. Our experiments show that both convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and vision transformers (ViTs) benefit from this approach, yielding higher test accuracy with faster convergence on several image classification benchmarks. Our method introduces negligible computational overhead and is compatible with common transfer learning pipelines.
2603.20860.paper_summary_zh: 轉移學習與在 ImageNet 上預訓練的模型已成為計算機視覺中的標準做法。<lb/>轉移學習是指在下游任務上微調預訓練神經網絡的權重，這些任務通常與 ImageNet 無關。<lb/>然而，預訓練的權重可能會變得飽和，並可能產生微不足道的梯度，無法適應下游任務。<lb/>這妨礙了模型有效訓練的能力，通常被稱為神經可塑性的喪失。<lb/>可塑性的喪失可能會阻止模型完全適應目標領域，特別是當下游數據集的性質不典型時。<lb/>雖然這個問題在持續學習中已被廣泛探討，但在轉移學習的背景下仍然相對不被重視。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們提出了一種有針對性的權重重新初始化策略，以在微調之前恢復神經可塑性。<lb/>我們的實驗表明，卷積神經網絡（CNNs）和視覺Transformer（ViTs）都從這種方法中受益，在幾個圖像分類基準上實現了更高的測試準確率和更快的收斂速度。<lb/>我們的方法引入的計算開銷微不足道，並且與常見的轉移學習流程兼容。
2603.20860.author: Xander Coetzer et.al.
2603.20860.authors: Xander Coetzer, Arné Schreuder, Anna Sergeevna Bosman
